explanations.1: The double colon :: after 'hello' means this target can be defined multiple times, and all versions will run when you call 'make hello'.
explanations.2: The @ symbol silences the echo command itself, so you only see the output message, not the command that produced it.
explanations.4: This is a second definition of the same 'hello' target; because of the double colons above, this will also run.
explanations.7: This is a third definition of 'hello'; all three recipe blocks (lines 2, 5, and 8) execute in order when you run 'make hello'.
